summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a high‑energy 10/8/89 show highlighted by a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s and soaring Bird Song & Morning Dew
review: Fans rave about this late‑'80s gem, praising the blistering first set and an even hotter second set anchored by one of the best Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequences ever. The SBD recording is described as crystal‑clear, with a punchy drum‑and‑bass mix that makes the performance feel immediate, while Brent’s keyboards and Jerry’s midi‑wired Wolf guitar add extra sparkle.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 enthusiastic reviews give 4‑5 stars, all highlighting stellar musical performance and a top‑tier soundboard recording; the weighted average yields an almost perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean SBD with exceptional drum/bass slap, clear midi‑guitar integration and a well‑balanced mix; reviewers call it the best SBD version and note the remaster sounds pristine.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑octane first set that builds to a massive energy burst on The Promised Land, followed by an unforgettable second set with seamless tuning moments leading into the iconic Help > Slip > Franklin’s and a soaring Morning Dew; overall pacing is tight and party‑like.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Long flowing hair, old Wolf guitar hooked up to a full MIDI rig for the whole show, stellar lead vocals on Franklin's and generally “gunna go off” playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass slap noted as a highlight; Phil and Brent revitalize the set during Gimme Some Lovin', keeping the energy high.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent is praised as “awesome,” owning the songs, especially on Help > Slip > Franklin’s and throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum mix is punchy; reviewers note “drums n' bass slap” and strong, driving rhythms.
